title: PayPal Q2 2024 Earnings Beat Expectations: 36% EPS Growth And Strong Revenue
text: PayPal Holdings reported strong second-quarter 2024 results that exceeded analyst expectations. The company delivered non-GAAP earnings of $1.19 per share, representing 36% year-over-year improvement and surpassing the Zacks consensus estimate by approximately 24%. Net revenues reached $7.9 billion, reflecting 8% growth on a reported basis and 9% on a currency-neutral basis, topping the Zacks estimate by just over 1%. Transaction revenues totaled $7.15 billion and grew 9% year-over-year, representing 91% of total revenues. U.S. revenues climbed to $4.55 billion (58% of total), up 8% annually, while international revenues reached $3.33 billion (42% of total), also rising 8%. Active accounts declined slightly by 0.5% to 429 million, yet exceeded consensus expectations of 428 million. Payment transactions grew 8% to 6.6 billion, though this missed the 6.9 billion consensus forecast. Total payment volume expanded 11% to $416.81 billion. Management projects mid-single-digit growth for Q3 revenues and anticipates low to mid-teens EPS growth for full-year 2024. The company expects $6 billion in free cash flow while returning $6 billion through share repurchases.
